program_overview.definition: Online/distance professional master's degree targeted at working adults looking to advance careers or become more marketable.
program_overview.capstone_thesis_requirement: None. Students complete research in classes but no thesis, independent study, or capstone project.

difference_from_ms.ms_thesis: Research-focused, on-campus, thesis required, funding available (RA). Not for online students.
difference_from_ms.ms_creative_component: 27 credits coursework + 3 credit capstone. No funding. Not for online students.
difference_from_ms.mhci_distance: Coursework-only (10 courses/30 credits), professional degree for online students. No difference from employer perspective.

application_process.who_can_apply: Applicants from all undergraduate majors, backgrounds, and work experiences.
application_process.response_time: 1-2 weeks after application is complete (all documents uploaded).
application_process.gpa_requirement: Accepts students with 2.8 GPA or higher.

prerequisites.programming: No prior experience needed to apply. Implementation/programming course required during program (levels available from beginner to advanced).

delivery_formats.asynchronous: flexible, no set meetings, weekly coursework on Canvas, group work coordinated by students.
delivery_formats.synchronous: live virtual lectures (schedule cannot be changed), typically evening (>5 PM CT), real-time discussions.

workload.recommendation: Full-time workers should take no more than 1-2 courses per semester.
workload.time_commitment: 6-12 hours per week per course (approx. 2 hours study per credit hour).
workload.pacing: Not self-paced. Deadlines must be met.

course_details.curriculum_flexibility: Courses can be taken in any order; core courses do not need to be first.

financial_info.assistantships: Not available to online students. (On-campus PhD/MS Thesis students typically receive them).
financial_info.scholarships: HCI Graduate Program does not fund scholarships. Students directed to ISU Office of Student Financial Aid.

career_info.internships: Not required but strongly encouraged. Graduate College Career Services can assist.
career_info.job_placement: No direct assistance/placement offered by program.
career_info.industry_context: Graduates work in interdisciplinary teams involving technology, design, engineering, and psychology.

conditions: Acceptable toward advanced degree at source institution
transfer_credits.process_timing: May need to wait until first semester or later to officially declare.
